bio.extra_names: 'The Father of Our National Parks'; 'Father of National Parks'

sources.short: West Adams Heritage Association — John Daggett Hooker biography
Robert Michael Pyle, foreword to 'John Muir's Last Journey'
Wikipedia: Marian Osgood Hooker (Mt Whitney 1903 climb)

careers.notes: Founder Sierra Club (1892, founding president until death). Authored numerous books on Sierra Nevada wilderness, glaciers, etc. Major influence on creation of Yosemite National Park (1890), Sequoia National Park (1890), and the National Park system.

notable.notable_blurb: Naturalist; founded Sierra Club; 'Father of National Parks'

narrative_blocks.header: Wrote his late books in the Hookers' garret
narrative_blocks.body: He returned four times to Los Angeles between 1910 and 1911 to write at the West Adams home of his friend John Daggett Hooker. Most of his major late writing was done in the Hooker garret.

narrative_blocks.header: Guided Marian to first-woman summit of Whitney
narrative_blocks.body: He guided a small Sierra party up Mt. Whitney in 1903 with Marian Osgood Hooker among them. She became the first woman to summit the peak named for her maternal great-uncle Josiah Dwight Whitney.

narrative_blocks.header: Wrote his most expressive late letters to Katharine
narrative_blocks.body: After his friend John Daggett Hooker's May 1911 death he wrote his most personal late letters to widow Katharine from his journeys to South America and Africa. The Bancroft Library at UC Berkeley preserves them as 'John Muir Letters to Katharine Putnam Hooker.'

narrative_blocks.header: Mourned a friend like a fallen leaf
narrative_blocks.body: After John Daggett Hooker's May 1911 death Muir wrote his own daughter Helen: 'I wonder if leaves feel lonely when they see their neighbors falling.' He died of pneumonia in Los Angeles three years later.

cross_connections.display_label: Wrote his late books in his West Adams garret.
Led the 1903 climb where she first-summited Mt. Whitney.
Wrote his most expressive late letters to her after widowing.
